problem_statement: Flight search by origin airport returns no results when the query uses a lower-case airport code (e.g. origin=ath), even though matching flights exist and airport codes are stored in upper case.
goal: Make origin-based flight search case-insensitive so that origin filters match airport codes regardless of the casing supplied by the client.
acceptance_criteria: GET /flights/search?origin=ath returns flights whose origin airport code is ATH.
Search behavior remains correct for already-uppercase inputs.
Only origin filtering behavior is changed; other flight search filters continue to work as before.
constraints: Likely fix should be localized to backend/app/services/flight_service.py in search_flights.
Airport codes are stored in upper case, so input normalization or case-insensitive comparison is needed.
unknowns: Whether search_flights also handles destination filtering and should use the same normalization pattern for consistency.
Whether there are existing tests covering case-insensitive search for origin.
repo_summary: Backend service-centric repo with flight API/routes, models, schemas, seed scripts, and service tests; issue explicitly points to backend/app/services/flight_service.py search_flights.

targeted_symbols.reason: This is the affected method called out by the issue; the empty results likely come from a case-sensitive comparison against upper-case stored airport codes.
Add or adjust tests to cover lower-case origin input and confirm case-insensitive matching.

localization_rationale: The issue reproduces on the search endpoint with an origin query parameter in lower case.
The expected behavior explicitly describes a casing mismatch between query input and stored airport codes.
The issue already names the implementation area: backend/app/services/flight_service.py (search_flights).
The smallest likely fix is to normalize the origin filter input or compare case-insensitively inside search_flights, with a corresponding service test.
